title: Scientists Found a 400-Year-Old Sea Giant in Fiordland
script: It predates your nation? Divers in Fiordland National Park, New Zealand, just documented the largest black coral colony ever recorded in New Zealand waters. It stands four meters tall and stretches four and a half meters wide. Scientists estimate it is between 300 and 400 years old, meaning it was alive in the 1620s, before the United States, before Australia was colonized, growing silently in the dark fjord water this entire time. Black coral grows only millimeters per year, so an animal this size is essentially irreplaceable on any human timeline. Fiordland's fjords protect it from wave action, which is why giants like this can survive there and almost nowhere else on Earth. So what else is hiding in Fiordland?

shots.phrase: Divers discover the ancient giant in Fiordland
